title: loeselia glandulosa subsp. cervantesii
intro: Loeselia glandulosa subsp. cervantesii, also known as Mountain-loeelia, is a flowering plant native to North America, particularly in the western regions, characterized by its fragrant purple flowers. Medicinally, Loeselia glandulosa subsp. cervantesii has been used for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and providing antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Loeselia glandulosa subsp. cervantesii can be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for a convenient and accessible way to harness its medicinal properties.
